River Water Quality Nitrogen Trends

Headline finding

River nitrogen trend monitoring shows variable trajectories across sites and assessment windows, with trend direction and coverage depending on parameter and period.

Why this matters for councils

River nitrogen trend indicators help target catchments where deteriorating water quality may require tighter source controls, restoration investment, or more focused monitoring.

Interactive overview

Code 
library(sf)
library(dplyr)
library(plotly)
library(leaflet)

gpkg_path <- "../data/river-water-quality/river-water-quality-nitrogen-trends-1991-2020.gpkg"

layers <- st_layers(gpkg_path)
rwq <- st_read(gpkg_path, layer = layers$name[1], quiet = TRUE)
rwq_tbl <- st_drop_geometry(rwq)

pick_col <- function(df, candidates) {
  hits <- intersect(candidates, names(df))
  if (length(hits) == 0) NA_character_ else hits[1]
}

parameter_col <- pick_col(
  rwq_tbl,
  c("parameter", "Parameter", "indicator", "Indicator", "np_id_name", "np_id", "attribute")
)
period_col <- pick_col(
  rwq_tbl,
  c("period", "Period", "trend_period", "window", "time_window", "assessment_period")
)
trend_col <- pick_col(
  rwq_tbl,
  c("trend", "Trend", "trend_direction", "direction", "Direction", "classification", "trend_class")
)

if (!is.na(trend_col) && !is.na(period_col)) {
  trend_profile <- rwq_tbl |>
    mutate(
      trend_band = as.character(.data[[trend_col]]),
      period_band = as.character(.data[[period_col]])
    ) |>
    filter(!is.na(trend_band), !is.na(period_band), trend_band != "", period_band != "") |>
    count(period_band, trend_band, name = "records")

  plot_ly(
    trend_profile,
    x = ~period_band,
    y = ~records,
    color = ~trend_band,
    type = "bar"
  ) |>
    layout(
      barmode = "stack",
      xaxis = list(title = "Assessment period"),
      yaxis = list(title = "Number of site records"),
      legend = list(title = list(text = "Trend direction"))
    )
} else if (!is.na(trend_col)) {
  trend_profile <- rwq_tbl |>
    mutate(trend_band = as.character(.data[[trend_col]])) |>
    filter(!is.na(trend_band), trend_band != "") |>
    count(trend_band, name = "records")

  plot_ly(
    trend_profile,
    x = ~reorder(trend_band, records),
    y = ~records,
    type = "bar",
    marker = list(color = "#2b7a78")
  ) |>
    layout(
      xaxis = list(title = "Trend direction"),
      yaxis = list(title = "Number of site records"),
      margin = list(b = 120)
    )
} else {
  fallback_col <- ifelse(is.na(parameter_col), names(rwq_tbl)[1], parameter_col)

  coverage_profile <- rwq_tbl |>
    mutate(group_name = as.character(.data[[fallback_col]])) |>
    filter(!is.na(group_name), group_name != "") |>
    count(group_name, sort = TRUE, name = "records") |>
    slice_head(n = 12)

  plot_ly(
    coverage_profile,
    x = ~reorder(group_name, records),
    y = ~records,
    type = "bar",
    marker = list(color = "#2b7a78")
  ) |>
    layout(
      xaxis = list(title = "Indicator field"),
      yaxis = list(title = "Number of records"),
      margin = list(b = 160)
    )
}
Code 
rwq_map <- rwq


# convert dataframe to sf object: coerce coords to numeric and remove invalid rows
rwq_map$long_num <- as.numeric(rwq_map$long)
rwq_map$lat_num <- as.numeric(rwq_map$lat)
# drop rows with NA coords (including non-numeric coercion results)
rwq_map <- rwq_map[!is.na(rwq_map$long_num) & !is.na(rwq_map$lat_num), ]
rwq_map <- st_as_sf(rwq_map, coords = c("long_num", "lat_num"), crs = 4326, remove = TRUE)


if (nrow(rwq_map) > 0) {
  rwq_map <- suppressWarnings(st_transform(rwq_map, 4326))
}

rwq_map_tbl <- st_drop_geometry(rwq_map)

site_col <- pick_col(rwq_map_tbl, c("site_name", "site", "s_id", "site_id", "location", "name"))
parameter_col <- pick_col(rwq_map_tbl, c("parameter", "Parameter", "indicator", "np_id_name", "np_id"))
period_col <- pick_col(rwq_map_tbl, c("period", "Period", "trend_period", "window", "assessment_period"))
trend_col <- pick_col(rwq_map_tbl, c("trend", "Trend", "trend_direction", "direction", "classification", "trend_class"))
value_col <- pick_col(rwq_map_tbl, c("median", "result", "Result", "value", "Value", "slope", "Sen_slope", "sen_slope"))

popup_fields <- unique(na.omit(c(site_col, parameter_col, period_col, trend_col, value_col)))

if (length(popup_fields) == 0) {
  popup_text <- rep("River monitoring site", nrow(rwq_map_tbl))
} else {
  popup_text <- apply(
    rwq_map_tbl[, popup_fields, drop = FALSE],
    1,
    function(x) {
      vals <- ifelse(is.na(x) | x == "", "NA", as.character(x))
      paste0(names(x), ": ", vals, collapse = "<br>")
    }
  )
}

leaflet(rwq_map) |>
  addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) |>
  addCircleMarkers(
    radius = 4,
    stroke = FALSE,
    fillOpacity = 0.55,
    color = "#ff7a59",
    popup = ~paste0(
      "measure: ", "measure",
      "<br>units: ", units,
      "<br>median: ", median,
      "<br>sen_lci: ", "sen_lci",
      "<br>trend: ", "trend_confidence"
    )
  )

Methods and source

  • Source: Ministry for the Environment, River water quality nitrogen trends (1991-2020).
  • Files used: data/river-water-quality/river-water-quality-nitrogen-trends-1991-2020.gpkg and data/river-water-quality/river-water-quality-nitrogen-trends-1991-2020.txt.
  • License: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.
  • Caveat: trend and parameter fields can vary by schema updates; this page emphasizes exploratory structure and pattern coverage rather than formal compliance assessment.
